title: 200 Ariary / 1000 Francs

obverse.description:
Old fortified village with a circular stone gate
village of the high plateaus in background

reverse.description: View of Aloalo Madagascar carved funeral posts. Often with carved Zebu heads. Zebu skulls placed on the graves. Honours important people of Mahafaly peoples in south western Madagascar.
reverse.lettering:
BANKY FOIBEN'I
MADAGASIKARA

200 ARIARY

ROAN-JATO ARIARY

comments:
The note also includes other security features, including:
Micro-print and faint yellow circles printed along right and left edges of the note;
Note value (200) is embossed on obverse, in the off-colored edge-strip along the right;
Edge-strip on reverse (left and right) has micro-print of the issuing authority (BANKY FOIBEN'IMADAGASIKARA) in text which alternates line by line right-side up & up-side down.
In a strip on the left reverse it is overprinted in green text with a warning against counterfeiting in two languages (Malagasy & French) as follows:
Helohin'ny Lalana Hiasa Anterivozona -----------
Mandrapahafatiny Ny Mpanao Vola Sandoka La Loi Punit De
Travaux Forces À Perpétuité Tout Contrefacteur De Monnaie

 

Below is a magnified view of the microprint that is seen under backlight. It has “BFM” and “200” repeated on it in green, with an outline of Madagascar interspersed in red:

 

The note has a green iridescent ink strip on the reverse. It has “200” and a block pattern repeated on it:

 

UV activity on a P#87b note at 365nm:
 
© Brandon Bertolli

watermark.description: Includes the head of a Zebu (also known as Indicine Cattle or Humped Cattle) and the note's value (200).
